title: santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. brevicaulis
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. brevicaulis, also known as the short-leaved grey cotton or short-leaved lavender cotton, is a shrubby perennial plant with silvery-gray foliage and small yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Santolina chamaecyparissus subvar. brevicaulis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, or used as a dried herb in potpourri blends and other herbal remedies.
